<td colspan="3" class="bgColor-muted p-1 rounded-top-2"><div class="LatestCommit-module__Box__B25ZT">
<h2 id="latest-commit" class="sr-only ScreenReaderHeading-module__userSelectNone__rwWIk prc-Heading-Heading-MtWFE" data-component="Heading" data-testid="screen-reader-heading">Latest commit</h2>

&#10;</div>

## Repository files navigation

- <a href="#" class="prc-components-UnderlineItem-7fP-n" aria-current="page"><span data-component="icon"><img src="data:image/svg+xml;base64," class="octicon octicon-book" /></span><span data-component="text" data-content="README">README</span></a>

<div class="js-snippet-clipboard-copy-unpositioned DirectoryRichtextContent-module__SharedMarkdownContent__hHXUL" hpc="true" containertiming="hpc">

<div class="markdown-heading" dir="auto">

# Ceph - a scalable distributed storage system

<a href="#ceph---a-scalable-distributed-storage-system" id="user-content-ceph---a-scalable-distributed-storage-system" class="anchor" aria-label="Permalink: Ceph - a scalable distributed storage system"><img src="data:image/svg+xml;base64," class="octicon octicon-link" /></a>

</div>

See <a href="https://ceph.com/" rel="nofollow">https://ceph.com/</a> for current information about Ceph.

<div class="markdown-heading" dir="auto">

## Status

<a href="#status" id="user-content-status" class="anchor" aria-label="Permalink: Status"><img src="data:image/svg+xml;base64," class="octicon octicon-link" /></a>

</div>

<a href="https://www.bestpractices.dev/projects/2220" rel="nofollow"><img src="https://camo.githubusercontent.com/16503b8998b1e351eb3975c15ee5725786a3db741ae15ca901ef9e50d224d0f2/68747470733a2f2f7777772e626573747072616374696365732e6465762f70726f6a656374732f323232302f6261646765" style="max-width: 100%;" data-canonical-src="https://www.bestpractices.dev/projects/2220/badge" alt="OpenSSF Best Practices" /></a> [<img src="https://github.com/ceph/ceph/actions/workflows/create-backport-trackers.yml/badge.svg" style="max-width: 100%;" alt="Issue Backporting" />](https://github.com/ceph/ceph/actions/workflows/create-backport-trackers.yml)

<div class="markdown-heading" dir="auto">

## Contributing Code

<a href="#contributing-code" id="user-content-contributing-code" class="anchor" aria-label="Permalink: Contributing Code"><img src="data:image/svg+xml;base64," class="octicon octicon-link" /></a>

</div>

Most of Ceph is dual-licensed under the LGPL version 2.1 or 3.0. Some miscellaneous code is either public domain or licensed under a BSD-style license.

The Ceph documentation is licensed under Creative Commons Attribution Share Alike 3.0 (CC-BY-SA-3.0).

Some headers included in the `ceph/ceph` repository are licensed under the GPL. See the file `COPYING` for a full inventory of licenses by file.

All code contributions must include a valid "Signed-off-by" line. See the file `SubmittingPatches.rst` for details on this and instructions on how to generate and submit patches.

Assignment of copyright is not required to contribute code. Code is contributed under the terms of the applicable license.

<div class="markdown-heading" dir="auto">

## Checking out the source

<a href="#checking-out-the-source" id="user-content-checking-out-the-source" class="anchor" aria-label="Permalink: Checking out the source"><img src="data:image/svg+xml;base64," class="octicon octicon-link" /></a>

</div>

Clone the ceph/ceph repository from github by running the following command on a system that has git installed:

<div class="snippet-clipboard-content notranslate position-relative overflow-auto" snippet-clipboard-copy-content="git clone git@github.com:ceph/ceph">

``` notranslate
git clone git@github.com:ceph/ceph
```

</div>

Alternatively, if you are not a github user, you should run the following command on a system that has git installed:

<div class="snippet-clipboard-content notranslate position-relative overflow-auto" snippet-clipboard-copy-content="git clone https://github.com/ceph/ceph.git">

``` notranslate
git clone https://github.com/ceph/ceph.git
```

</div>

When the `ceph/ceph` repository has been cloned to your system, run the following commands to move into the cloned `ceph/ceph` repository and to check out the git submodules associated with it:

<div class="snippet-clipboard-content notranslate position-relative overflow-auto" snippet-clipboard-copy-content="cd ceph
git submodule update --init --recursive --progress">

``` notranslate
cd ceph
git submodule update --init --recursive --progress
```

</div>

<div class="markdown-heading" dir="auto">

## Build Prerequisites

<a href="#build-prerequisites" id="user-content-build-prerequisites" class="anchor" aria-label="Permalink: Build Prerequisites"><img src="data:image/svg+xml;base64," class="octicon octicon-link" /></a>

</div>

*section last updated 06 Sep 2024*

We provide the Debian and Ubuntu `apt` commands in this procedure. If you use a system with a different package manager, then you will have to use different commands.

#\. Install `curl`:

<div class="snippet-clipboard-content notranslate position-relative overflow-auto" snippet-clipboard-copy-content="apt install curl">

``` notranslate
apt install curl
```

</div>

#\. Install package dependencies by running the `install-deps.sh` script:

<div class="snippet-clipboard-content notranslate position-relative overflow-auto" snippet-clipboard-copy-content="./install-deps.sh">

``` notranslate
./install-deps.sh
```

</div>

#\. Install the `python3-routes` package:

<div class="snippet-clipboard-content notranslate position-relative overflow-auto" snippet-clipboard-copy-content="apt install python3-routes">

``` notranslate
apt install python3-routes
```

</div>

<div class="markdown-heading" dir="auto">

## Building Ceph

<a href="#building-ceph" id="user-content-building-ceph" class="anchor" aria-label="Permalink: Building Ceph"><img src="data:image/svg+xml;base64," class="octicon octicon-link" /></a>

</div>

These instructions are meant for developers who are compiling the code for development and testing. To build binaries that are suitable for installation we recommend that you build `.deb` or `.rpm` packages, or refer to `ceph.spec.in` or `debian/rules` to see which configuration options are specified for production builds.

To build Ceph, follow this procedure:

1.  Make sure that you are in the top-level `ceph` directory that contains `do_cmake.sh` and `CONTRIBUTING.rst`.

2.  Run the `do_cmake.sh` script:

    <div class="snippet-clipboard-content notranslate position-relative overflow-auto" snippet-clipboard-copy-content="./do_cmake.sh">

    ``` notranslate
    ./do_cmake.sh
    ```

    </div>

    See [build types](#build-types).

3.  Move into the `build` directory:

    <div class="snippet-clipboard-content notranslate position-relative overflow-auto" snippet-clipboard-copy-content="cd build">

    ``` notranslate
    cd build
    ```

    </div>

4.  Use the `ninja` buildsystem to build the development environment:

    <div class="snippet-clipboard-content notranslate position-relative overflow-auto" snippet-clipboard-copy-content="ninja -j3">

    ``` notranslate
    ninja -j3
    ```

    </div>

    > \[!IMPORTANT\]
    >
    > <a href="https://ninja-build.org/" rel="nofollow">Ninja</a> is the build system used by the Ceph project to build test builds. The number of jobs used by `ninja` is derived from the number of CPU cores of the building host if unspecified. Use the `-j` option to limit the job number if build jobs are running out of memory. If you attempt to run `ninja` and receive a message that reads `g++: fatal error: Killed signal terminated program cc1plus`, then you have run out of memory.
    >
    > Using the `-j` option with an argument appropriate to the hardware on which the `ninja` command is run is expected to result in a successful build. For example, to limit the job number to 3, run the command `ninja -j3`. On average, each `ninja` job run in parallel needs approximately 2.5 GiB of RAM.

    This documentation assumes that your build directory is a subdirectory of the `ceph.git` checkout. If the build directory is located elsewhere, point `CEPH_GIT_DIR` to the correct path of the checkout. Additional CMake args can be specified by setting ARGS before invoking `do_cmake.sh`. See [cmake options](#cmake-options) for more details. For example:

    <div class="snippet-clipboard-content notranslate position-relative overflow-auto" snippet-clipboard-copy-content="ARGS=&quot;-DCMAKE_C_COMPILER=gcc-7&quot; ./do_cmake.sh">

    ``` notranslate
    ARGS="-DCMAKE_C_COMPILER=gcc-7" ./do_cmake.sh
    ```

    </div>

    To build only certain targets, run a command of the following form:

    <div class="snippet-clipboard-content notranslate position-relative overflow-auto" snippet-clipboard-copy-content="ninja [target name]">

    ``` notranslate
    ninja [target name]
    ```

    </div>

5.  Install the vstart cluster:

    <div class="snippet-clipboard-content notranslate position-relative overflow-auto" snippet-clipboard-copy-content="ninja install">

    ``` notranslate
    ninja install
    ```

    </div>

<div class="markdown-heading" dir="auto">

## Build Types

<a href="#build-types" id="user-content-build-types" class="anchor" aria-label="Permalink: Build Types"><img src="data:image/svg+xml;base64," class="octicon octicon-link" /></a>

</div>

`do_cmake.sh` by default creates a "debug build" of Ceph (assuming `.git` exists). A `Debug` build runtime performance may be as little as 20% of that of a non-debug build. Pass `-DCMAKE_BUILD_TYPE=RelWithDebInfo` to `do_cmake.sh` to create a non-debug build. The default build type is `RelWithDebInfo` once `.git` does not exist.

| CMake mode | Debug info | Optimizations | Sanitizers | Checks | Use for |
|----|----|----|----|----|----|
| `Debug` | Yes | `-Og` | None | `ceph_assert`, `assert` | gdb, development |
| `RelWithDebInfo` | Yes | `-O2`, `-DNDEBUG` | None | `ceph_assert` only | production |

<div class="markdown-heading" dir="auto">

### CMake Options

<a href="#cmake-options" id="user-content-cmake-options" class="anchor" aria-label="Permalink: CMake Options"><img src="data:image/svg+xml;base64," class="octicon octicon-link" /></a>

</div>

The `-D` flag can be used with `cmake` to speed up the process of building Ceph and to customize the build.

<div class="markdown-heading" dir="auto">

#### Building without RADOS Gateway

<a href="#building-without-rados-gateway" id="user-content-building-without-rados-gateway" class="anchor" aria-label="Permalink: Building without RADOS Gateway"><img src="data:image/svg+xml;base64," class="octicon octicon-link" /></a>

</div>

The RADOS Gateway is built by default. To build Ceph without the RADOS Gateway, run a command of the following form:

<div class="snippet-clipboard-content notranslate position-relative overflow-auto" snippet-clipboard-copy-content="cmake -DWITH_RADOSGW=OFF [path to top-level ceph directory]">

``` notranslate
cmake -DWITH_RADOSGW=OFF [path to top-level ceph directory]
```

</div>

<div class="markdown-heading" dir="auto">

#### Building with debugging and arbitrary dependency locations

<a href="#building-with-debugging-and-arbitrary-dependency-locations" id="user-content-building-with-debugging-and-arbitrary-dependency-locations" class="anchor" aria-label="Permalink: Building with debugging and arbitrary dependency locations"><img src="data:image/svg+xml;base64," class="octicon octicon-link" /></a>

</div>

Run a command of the following form to build Ceph with debugging and alternate locations for some external dependencies:

<div class="snippet-clipboard-content notranslate position-relative overflow-auto" snippet-clipboard-copy-content="cmake -DCMAKE_INSTALL_PREFIX=/opt/ceph -DCMAKE_C_FLAGS=&quot;-Og -g3 -gdwarf-4&quot; \
..">

``` notranslate
cmake -DCMAKE_INSTALL_PREFIX=/opt/ceph -DCMAKE_C_FLAGS="-Og -g3 -gdwarf-4" \
..
```

</div>

Ceph has several bundled dependencies such as Boost, RocksDB and Arrow. By default, `cmake` builds these bundled dependencies from source instead of using libraries that are already installed on the system. You can opt to use these system libraries, as long as they meet Ceph's version requirements. To use system libraries, use `cmake` options like `WITH_SYSTEM_BOOST`, as in the following example:

<div class="snippet-clipboard-content notranslate position-relative overflow-auto" snippet-clipboard-copy-content="cmake -DWITH_SYSTEM_BOOST=ON [...]">

``` notranslate
cmake -DWITH_SYSTEM_BOOST=ON [...]
```

</div>

To view an exhaustive list of -D options, invoke `cmake -LH`:

<div class="snippet-clipboard-content notranslate position-relative overflow-auto" snippet-clipboard-copy-content="cmake -LH">

``` notranslate
cmake -LH
```

</div>

<div class="markdown-heading" dir="auto">

#### Preserving diagnostic colors

<a href="#preserving-diagnostic-colors" id="user-content-preserving-diagnostic-colors" class="anchor" aria-label="Permalink: Preserving diagnostic colors"><img src="data:image/svg+xml;base64," class="octicon octicon-link" /></a>

</div>

If you pipe `ninja` to `less` and would like to preserve the diagnostic colors in the output in order to make errors and warnings more legible, run the following command:

<div class="snippet-clipboard-content notranslate position-relative overflow-auto" snippet-clipboard-copy-content="cmake -DDIAGNOSTICS_COLOR=always ...">

``` notranslate
cmake -DDIAGNOSTICS_COLOR=always ...
```

</div>

The above command works only with supported compilers.

The diagnostic colors will be visible when the following command is run:

<div class="snippet-clipboard-content notranslate position-relative overflow-auto" snippet-clipboard-copy-content="ninja | less -R">

``` notranslate
ninja | less -R
```

</div>

Other available values for `DIAGNOSTICS_COLOR` are `auto` (default) and `never`.

<div class="markdown-heading" dir="auto">

## Tips and Tricks

<a href="#tips-and-tricks" id="user-content-tips-and-tricks" class="anchor" aria-label="Permalink: Tips and Tricks"><img src="data:image/svg+xml;base64," class="octicon octicon-link" /></a>

</div>

- Use "debug builds" only when needed. Debugging builds are helpful for development, but they can slow down performance. Use `-DCMAKE_BUILD_TYPE=Release` when debugging isn't necessary.
- Enable Selective Daemons when testing specific components. Don't start unnecessary daemons.
- Preserve Existing Data skip cluster reinitialization between tests by using the `-n` flag.
- To manage a vstart cluster, stop daemons using `./stop.sh` and start them with `./vstart.sh --daemon osd.${ID} [--nodaemonize]`.
- Restart the sockets by stopping and restarting the daemons associated with them. This ensures that there are no stale sockets in the cluster.
- To track RocksDB performance, set `export ROCKSDB_PERF=true` and start the cluster by using the command `./vstart.sh -n -d -x --bluestore`.
- Build with `vstart-base` using debug flags in cmake, compile, and deploy via `./vstart.sh -d -n --bluestore`.
- To containerize, generate configurations with `vstart.sh`, and deploy with Docker, mapping directories and configuring the network.
- Manage containers using `docker run`, `stop`, and `rm`. For detailed setups, consult the Ceph-Container repository.

<div class="markdown-heading" dir="auto">

## Troubleshooting

<a href="#troubleshooting" id="user-content-troubleshooting" class="anchor" aria-label="Permalink: Troubleshooting"><img src="data:image/svg+xml;base64," class="octicon octicon-link" /></a>

</div>

- Cluster Fails to Start: Look for errors in the logs under the `out/` directory.
- OSD Crashes: Check the OSD logs for errors.
- Cluster in a `Health Error` State: Run the `ceph status` command to identify the issue.
- RocksDB Errors: Look for RocksDB-related errors in the OSD logs.

<div class="markdown-heading" dir="auto">

## Building a source tarball

<a href="#building-a-source-tarball" id="user-content-building-a-source-tarball" class="anchor" aria-label="Permalink: Building a source tarball"><img src="data:image/svg+xml;base64," class="octicon octicon-link" /></a>

</div>

To build a complete source tarball with everything needed to build from source and/or build a (deb or rpm) package, run

<div class="snippet-clipboard-content notranslate position-relative overflow-auto" snippet-clipboard-copy-content="./make-dist">

``` notranslate
./make-dist
```

</div>

This will create a tarball like ceph-\$version.tar.bz2 from git. (Ensure that any changes you want to include in your working directory are committed to git.)

<div class="markdown-heading" dir="auto">

## Running a test cluster

<a href="#running-a-test-cluster" id="user-content-running-a-test-cluster" class="anchor" aria-label="Permalink: Running a test cluster"><img src="data:image/svg+xml;base64," class="octicon octicon-link" /></a>

</div>

From the `ceph/` directory, run the following commands to launch a test Ceph cluster:

<div class="snippet-clipboard-content notranslate position-relative overflow-auto" snippet-clipboard-copy-content="cd build
ninja vstart        # builds just enough to run vstart
../src/vstart.sh --debug --new -x --localhost --bluestore
./bin/ceph -s">

``` notranslate
cd build
ninja vstart        # builds just enough to run vstart
../src/vstart.sh --debug --new -x --localhost --bluestore
./bin/ceph -s
```

</div>

Most Ceph commands are available in the `bin/` directory. For example:

<div class="snippet-clipboard-content notranslate position-relative overflow-auto" snippet-clipboard-copy-content="./bin/rbd create foo --size 1000
./bin/rados -p foo bench 30 write">

``` notranslate
./bin/rbd create foo --size 1000
./bin/rados -p foo bench 30 write
```

</div>

To shut down the test cluster, run the following command from the `build/` directory:

<div class="snippet-clipboard-content notranslate position-relative overflow-auto" snippet-clipboard-copy-content="../src/stop.sh">

``` notranslate
../src/stop.sh
```

</div>

Use the sysvinit script to start or stop individual daemons:

<div class="snippet-clipboard-content notranslate position-relative overflow-auto" snippet-clipboard-copy-content="./bin/init-ceph restart osd.0
./bin/init-ceph stop">

``` notranslate
./bin/init-ceph restart osd.0
./bin/init-ceph stop
```

</div>

<div class="markdown-heading" dir="auto">

## Running unit tests

<a href="#running-unit-tests" id="user-content-running-unit-tests" class="anchor" aria-label="Permalink: Running unit tests"><img src="data:image/svg+xml;base64," class="octicon octicon-link" /></a>

</div>

To build and run all tests (in parallel using all processors), use `ctest`:

<div class="snippet-clipboard-content notranslate position-relative overflow-auto" snippet-clipboard-copy-content="cd build
ninja
ctest -j$(nproc)">

``` notranslate
cd build
ninja
ctest -j$(nproc)
```

</div>

(Note: Many targets built from src/test are not run using `ctest`. Targets starting with "unittest" are run in `ninja check` and thus can be run with `ctest`. Targets starting with "ceph_test" can not, and should be run by hand.)

When failures occur, look in build/Testing/Temporary for logs.

To build and run all tests and their dependencies without other unnecessary targets in Ceph:

<div class="snippet-clipboard-content notranslate position-relative overflow-auto" snippet-clipboard-copy-content="cd build
ninja check -j$(nproc)">

``` notranslate
cd build
ninja check -j$(nproc)
```

</div>

To run an individual test manually, run `ctest` with -R (regex matching):

<div class="snippet-clipboard-content notranslate position-relative overflow-auto" snippet-clipboard-copy-content="ctest -R [regex matching test name(s)]">

``` notranslate
ctest -R [regex matching test name(s)]
```

</div>

(Note: `ctest` does not build the test it's running or the dependencies needed to run it)

To run an individual test manually and see all the tests output, run `ctest` with the -V (verbose) flag:

<div class="snippet-clipboard-content notranslate position-relative overflow-auto" snippet-clipboard-copy-content="ctest -V -R [regex matching test name(s)]">

``` notranslate
ctest -V -R [regex matching test name(s)]
```

</div>

To run tests manually and run the jobs in parallel, run `ctest` with the `-j` flag:

<div class="snippet-clipboard-content notranslate position-relative overflow-auto" snippet-clipboard-copy-content="ctest -j [number of jobs]">

``` notranslate
ctest -j [number of jobs]
```

</div>

There are many other flags you can give `ctest` for better control over manual test execution. To view these options run:

<div class="snippet-clipboard-content notranslate position-relative overflow-auto" snippet-clipboard-copy-content="man ctest">

``` notranslate
man ctest
```

</div>

<div class="markdown-heading" dir="auto">

### Building Ceph using Containers

<a href="#building-ceph-using-containers" id="user-content-building-ceph-using-containers" class="anchor" aria-label="Permalink: Building Ceph using Containers"><img src="data:image/svg+xml;base64," class="octicon octicon-link" /></a>

</div>

Ceph now provides tools to build the code, run unit tests, or build packages from within an OCI-style container using Podman or Docker! This allows one to build code for distributions other than the one you have on your system, avoids the need to install build dependencies for Ceph on your local system and provides an opportunity to test builds on platforms that are not yet supported by the official build infrastructure. For more details see the [container build document](/ceph/ceph/blob/main/ContainerBuild.md).

<div class="markdown-heading" dir="auto">

## Building the Documentation

<a href="#building-the-documentation" id="user-content-building-the-documentation" class="anchor" aria-label="Permalink: Building the Documentation"><img src="data:image/svg+xml;base64," class="octicon octicon-link" /></a>

</div>

<div class="markdown-heading" dir="auto">

### Prerequisites

<a href="#prerequisites" id="user-content-prerequisites" class="anchor" aria-label="Permalink: Prerequisites"><img src="data:image/svg+xml;base64," class="octicon octicon-link" /></a>

</div>

The list of package dependencies for building the documentation can be found in `doc_deps.deb.txt`:

<div class="snippet-clipboard-content notranslate position-relative overflow-auto" snippet-clipboard-copy-content="sudo apt-get install `cat doc_deps.deb.txt`">

``` notranslate
sudo apt-get install `cat doc_deps.deb.txt`
```

</div>

<div class="markdown-heading" dir="auto">

### Building the Documentation

<a href="#building-the-documentation-1" id="user-content-building-the-documentation-1" class="anchor" aria-label="Permalink: Building the Documentation"><img src="data:image/svg+xml;base64," class="octicon octicon-link" /></a>

</div>

To build the documentation, ensure that you are in the top-level `/ceph` directory, and execute the build script. For example:

<div class="snippet-clipboard-content notranslate position-relative overflow-auto" snippet-clipboard-copy-content="admin/build-doc">

``` notranslate
admin/build-doc
```

</div>

<div class="markdown-heading" dir="auto">

## Reporting Issues

<a href="#reporting-issues" id="user-content-reporting-issues" class="anchor" aria-label="Permalink: Reporting Issues"><img src="data:image/svg+xml;base64," class="octicon octicon-link" /></a>

</div>

To report an issue and view existing issues, please visit <a href="https://tracker.ceph.com/projects/ceph" rel="nofollow">https://tracker.ceph.com/projects/ceph</a>.

</div>

</div>

</div>

</div>

</div>

</div>

</div>

## About

Ceph is a distributed object, block, and file storage platform

</div>
